incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — supply chain attack group (TeamPCP)
incidents.vector: Compromised the Telnyx Python SDK on PyPI (670K+ monthly downloads) using stolen credentials and WAV audio file steganography for payload delivery.
incidents.impact: Malicious versions 4.87.1 and 4.87.2 deployed persistent backdoors on Windows and credential harvesters on Linux/macOS before being quarantined. Part of an escalating multi-target supply chain campaign.

incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — ransomware (TeamPCP + Vect RaaS + BreachForums)
incidents.vector: TeamPCP formally partnered with Vect ransomware-as-a-service and BreachForums, distributing personal affiliate keys to approximately 300,000 forum users.
incidents.impact: Potentially one of the largest coordinated ransomware affiliate mobilizations ever observed. TeamPCP provides initial access via supply chain compromises, Vect provides encryption and extortion tooling.

incidents.attacker: Iran — Ministry of Intelligence (MOIS) hacktivist proxies
incidents.vector: Compromise of the FBI director's personal email account and publication of contents by Iran-linked hacktivist group.
incidents.impact: Personal emails, documents, and photos from 2010 and 2019 exfiltrated and published online via Distributed Denial of Secrets. FBI confirmed the targeting but said information was historical with no government data.

incidents.vector: Claimed breach using credentials obtained via the TeamPCP supply chain campaign, allegedly gaining access to internal code repositories and cloud configurations.
incidents.impact: Claimed exfiltration of 3GB of data including internal code repos, AWS/Azure/Terraform cloud configs, GitHub Enterprise user info, and employee data. AstraZeneca has not confirmed or denied. Data being sold via encrypted messaging.

incidents.attacker: Iran — Ministry of Intelligence (MOIS) hacktivist proxies
incidents.vector: Iran-linked hacktivist group deployed data-wiping malware across the company's global infrastructure, defacing login pages.
incidents.impact: Claims of 200,000+ systems, servers, and devices wiped across 79 countries. Approximately 5,000 workers sent home in Ireland. Corporate headquarters reporting building emergency. Staff reduced to WhatsApp for communications.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of CVE-2026-33017 (CVSS 9.8 code injection) in Langflow open-source AI agent framework, exploited within hours of disclosure with no public PoC needed.
incidents.impact: Over 1,000 exploitation attempts observed including infostealers, reverse shells, and cryptominers. Attackers extracting API keys for OpenAI, Anthropic, and AWS from vulnerable instances. CISA added to KEV catalog March 25.

incidents.attacker: China — state-suspected espionage groups (three distinct clusters)
incidents.vector: Three separate China-aligned clusters simultaneously targeted one government organization using USB-propagated malware, custom backdoors, and novel loaders with fake TLS headers.
incidents.impact: Persistent espionage access established via multiple backdoors including PUBLOAD, CoolClient, EggStremeFuel, Masol RAT, and FluffyGh0st RAT. Three clusters likely coordinating toward long-term persistent access to sensitive government systems.

incidents.victim: Bearlyfy pro-Ukraine ransomware vs Russian businesses
incidents.vector: Pro-Ukrainian group deployed custom GenieLocker ransomware against Russian companies since early March 2026, previously using leaked LockBit 3 Black and Babuk code.
incidents.impact: Over 70 attacks on Russian companies since January 2025. Ransom demands have grown to hundreds of thousands of dollars. Collaboration with other pro-Ukrainian hacking groups.

incidents.vector: Armenian national Hambardzum Minasyan developed and sold RedLine infostealer malware since March 2020, enabling thousands of attacks across 150+ countries.
incidents.impact: Developer extradited to US and indicted on conspiracy charges (access device fraud, CFAA, money laundering), facing up to 30 years. Follows October 2024 infrastructure takedown. RedLine stole credentials, credit cards, and crypto wallets at massive scale.

incidents.victim: LeakBase forum (147,000+ users, hundreds of millions of stolen records)
incidents.vector: International law enforcement operation led by FBI with 100+ actions across 12+ countries targeting the LeakBase stolen data marketplace, operational since 2021.
incidents.impact: Russian administrator arrested in Taganrog. Forum facilitated trade in hundreds of millions of compromised records. Operated with a rule prohibiting sale of Russian data, suggesting operator ties to Russia.

incidents.vector: Ransomware attack disrupting operations at a major Spanish fishing port.
incidents.impact: Port operations disrupted. Limited details available.

incidents.vector: Cyberattack on Puerto Rico's Department of Transportation and Public Works forcing cancellation of driver's license appointments.
incidents.impact: All driver's license appointments cancelled territory-wide. Government services disrupted for citizens.

incidents.vector: Cyber breach affecting internal systems at the Netherlands Ministry of Finance.
incidents.impact: Internal systems compromised. Dutch government is actively investigating. Limited public details available.

incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — supply chain attack group (TeamPCP)
incidents.vector: Compromised the Checkmarx KICS GitHub Action (March 23, 12:58-16:50 UTC), injecting credential-stealing malware into the official security scanner. Possibly via credentials stolen from prior Trivy compromise.
incidents.impact: Organizations running KICS in CI/CD pipelines during the 4-hour window had credentials and secrets exfiltrated. Part of broader TeamPCP supply chain campaign.

incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — supply chain attack group (TeamPCP)
incidents.vector: Compromised the LiteLLM CEO's personal GitHub account and used it to publish malicious litellm==1.82.8 on PyPI with C2 callbacks, .pth auto-execution, and Kubernetes lateral movement.
incidents.impact: Malicious package deployed persistent backdoors, attempted privileged pod creation in Kubernetes clusters, and swept multi-cloud credentials. Forensic IOCs published including C2 domain models.litellm.cloud.

incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — supply chain attack group (TeamPCP)
incidents.vector: Compromised Aqua Security's Trivy GitHub Action via stolen personal access token, injecting credential-stealing malware into official releases. Deployed CanisterWorm self-propagating worm targeting Docker APIs, Kubernetes, and Redis.
incidents.impact: SSH keys, cloud credentials, Kubernetes tokens, and crypto wallets stolen from CI/CD environments. Weekend wiper payload specifically targeted Iranian systems (detecting timezone/locale). Azure (61%) and AWS (36%) account for 97% of compromised servers. CISA added CVE-2026-33634 to KEV catalog.

incidents.victim: DOJ botnet takedown (Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid, Mossad botnets)
incidents.vector: Four IoT botnets hijacked 3+ million devices (routers, cameras) via known vulnerabilities, launching DDoS attacks and demanding extortion payments.
incidents.impact: DOJ with Canada and Germany dismantled infrastructure. Over 315,000 attack commands issued collectively. DDoS attacks targeted US Department of Defense IP addresses. Extortion payments demanded from victims.

incidents.victim: Iran-related phishing targets (UAE, Saudi Arabia, Middle East)
incidents.attacker: Iran — state-aligned and opportunistic criminal groups
incidents.vector: Following the February 28 US-Israeli strikes, 7,381 conflict-themed phishing URLs across 1,881 hostnames targeting telecoms, airlines, law enforcement, and energy corporations with credential harvesting and financial fraud.
incidents.impact: Widespread brand impersonation of UAE airlines, Dubai government, Emirates Post, Saudi ERP providers, and Iranian banks. Credit card theft, banking credential harvesting, and StealC infostealer infrastructure deployed at scale.

incidents.victim: Israel, Gulf states, Cyprus (IP cameras and SCADA systems)
incidents.vector: Iran expanded scanning for exposed IP cameras and SCADA/PLC systems in Israel, Qatar, Bahrain, Kuwait, UAE, and Cyprus following revelation that US/Israel used compromised Iranian traffic cameras to track senior leadership.
incidents.impact: Shift to opportunistic attacks on any exposed device in target nations. Companies in these countries at risk of being caught in nation-state crossfire. Check Point confirmed increased Iranian reconnaissance activity.

incidents.victim: Healthcare, government, hospitality, and education sectors
incidents.vector: Infostealer hidden in fake copyright infringement notices distributed via email to organizations in critical sectors.
incidents.impact: Installs PureLog Stealer malware for credential and data theft. Campaign targeting multiple sectors simultaneously.

incidents.victim: iPhone users on older iOS versions
incidents.vector: Complete iPhone exploit kit (DarkSword) publicly leaked, capable of compromising devices running iOS versions prior to iOS 26.
incidents.impact: Researchers warn the toolkit could enable hacking of millions of iPhones still on older iOS versions. Lowers the barrier for both criminal and state-sponsored exploitation of Apple devices.

incidents.victim: US companies hiring remote IT workers
incidents.vector: North Korean IT workers using hardware IP KVM devices (Sipeed nano KVM, PiKVM) to remotely access laptops shipped to US addresses, evading software-based remote access detection.
incidents.impact: New tradecraft evolution bypasses traditional detection of remote desktop software. Revenue generated for North Korean regime while gaining potential insider access to corporate networks.
